title: [LTL] Add explict clocked operations and types
body:
This pr partially implement the issue #8673 ## LTL Dialect Explicit Clocking Model Refactoring ### Problem and Motivation The original LTL dialect uses implicit clock inference, violating IR locality principles. The `ltl.clock` operation creates non-local dependencies, hindering safe transformations. ### Type Hierarchy ``` i1 --> sequence ------------> property | | ↑ | | |(property level op) └-------┴-> clocked_sequence --┘ ``` ### Refactoring Principles **Input Unification**: All `clocked_*` operations accept `LTLClockedSequenceType` as input **Result Clock Rules**: - **Multi-operand operations**: Explicitly specify `ClockEdgeAttr` and `clock` parameters - **Unary operations**: Clock inherited from input - **Property operations**: Lift to clock-agnostic `LTLPropertyType` **Key Changes**: - `ltl.delay` → `ltl.clocked_delay`: From input-dependent to standalone delay object - `ltl.create_clocked_sequence`: Create clocked sequence instead of adding a clock to sequence ### Compatibility - Preserve legacy `sequence` operations for Immediate Assertion support - Enable new-old interoperability through `ltl.create_clocked_sequence` - Property-level methods' operand generics should add ClockedSequence in the next commit ### Implementation roadmap: 1. Implement clocked_* methods 2. Add ClockedSequence to operand generics of property-level methods (ltl.implication, ltl.until, ltl.eventually, ltl.not) 3. Implement LTLToCore lowering for clocked sequence 4. `clocked_sequence` would be rename to `sequence`, and `sequence` would be rename to `unclocked_sequence` 5. Add tests and complete documentation | Old Operation | Operands | Results | New Operation | Operands | Results | |---------------|----------|---------|---------------|----------|---------| | `ltl.clock`✅ | `input: LTLAnyPropertyType`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1` | `LTLSequenceOrPropertyType` | `ltl.create_clocked_sequence` | `input: LTLAnySequenceType`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.and`✅ | `inputs: Variadic` | `LTLAnyPropertyType` | `ltl.clocked_and` | `inputs: Variadic`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.or`✅ | `inputs: Variadic` | `LTLAnyPropertyType` | `ltl.clocked_or` | `inputs: Variadic`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.intersect`✅ | `inputs: Variadic` | `LTLPropertyType` | `ltl.clocked_intersect` | `inputs: Variadic`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.concat`✅ | `inputs: Variadic` | `LTLSequenceType` | `ltl.clocked_concat` | `inputs: Variadic`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.delay`❌ | `input: LTLAnySequenceType`
`delay: I64Attr`
`length: OptionalAttr` | `LTLSequenceType` | `ltl.clocked_delay` | `input: LTLClockedSequenceType`
`edge: ClockEdgeAttr`
`clock: I1`
`delay: I64Attr`
`length: OptionalAttr` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.repeat`✅ | `input: LTLAnySequenceType`
`base: I64Attr`
`more: OptionalAttr` | `LTLSequenceType` | `ltl.clocked_repeat` | `input: LTLClockedSequenceType`
`base: I64Attr`
`more: OptionalAttr` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.goto_repeat`✅ | `input: LTLAnySequenceType`
`base: I64Attr`
`more: I64Attr` | `LTLSequenceType` | `ltl.clocked_goto_repeat` | `input: LTLClockedSequenceType`
`base: I64Attr`
`more: I64Attr` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | | `ltl.non_consecutive_repeat`✅ | `input: LTLAnySequenceType`
`base: I64Attr`
`more: I64Attr` | `LTLSequenceType` | `ltl.clocked_non_consecutive_repeat` | `input: LTLClockedSequenceType`
`base: I64Attr`
`more: I64Attr` | `LTLClockedSequenceType` | ✅ indicates retention, ❌ indicates removal
